symptoms.symptom_name: Exertional central chest tightness relieved by rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Effort-precipitated, rest-relieved retrosternal tightness is the defining feature of angina.
Reflux is typically post-prandial/positional, not strictly effort-related.

symptoms.symptom_name: Radiation of chest pain to jaw and left arm

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Radiation to jaw/arm substantially increases the likelihood that chest pain is ischaemic.
New-onset or accelerating effort angina defines the unstable pattern within the ACS spectrum.
Stability requires an unchanging threshold over weeks-months; a 48-hour crescendo course is by definition not stable.
Autonomic symptoms such as nausea commonly accompany myocardial ischaemia.
Breathlessness on exertion is an anginal equivalent and marks a larger ischaemic burden.
Smoking is a major independent risk factor for coronary artery disease, raising pre-test probability.
Dyslipidaemia drives atherogenesis; poor adherence removes a protective factor.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain not meal-related, not positional, and not reproducible on palpation or by breathing/movement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Musculoskeletal pain is usually reproducible on palpation or altered by movement/respiration.
Reflux pain tends to be post-prandial or worse lying down, absent here.

symptoms.symptom_name: S4 gallop at the apex on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An S4 reflects a stiff, poorly compliant ischaemic left ventricle — a clinician-only auscultatory finding, sealed as unavailable via telehealth.
Clinician-measured vitals — mild hypertension adds cardiovascular risk context but is non-diagnostic; not obtainable via telehealth without a validated home device.

investigation_details.expected_result: May show ischaemic ST-T changes or be normal between episodes; a normal ECG does not exclude unstable angina
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: An evolving MI or dynamic ischaemia can be missed

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The ECG is first-line for triaging chest pain to STEMI/NSTEMI/unstable angina; unavailable via telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Distinguishes unstable angina (normal troponin) from NSTEMI (rising troponin)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Troponin is the biomarker that separates infarction from unstable angina; only obtainable in person.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Crescendo exertional ischaemic pain with radiation and risk factors
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Unstable angina / acute coronary syndrome spectrum
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these mark a new-onset, accelerating ischaemic syndrome that must be triaged as possible ACS, not stable angina or a benign mimic.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line triage of suspected ACS; must be done in person as it cannot be obtained via telehealth.
Excludes/confirms myocardial infarction; the central reason in-person assessment is required today.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Cardiology referral for stress echocardiography or coronary angiography
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Downstream functional/anatomical risk stratification once acute MI is excluded.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT coronary angiogram as a first test in a stable, currently pain-free patient with no red flags at presentation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not the correct first step here — the accelerating, unstable pattern needs same-day ECG and troponin, not an outpatient elective scan; ordering it in place of urgent assessment would be an over-investigation that delays escalation.
